conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, let me read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, **do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex, who's 13 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information! You have one child, Alex, who is 13 years old.

Now I need to ask you a few questions to determine your eligibility:

**Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. Alex lives at home with me here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great. So Alex lives with you in the UK.

Now I need to ask: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you or your partner (if you have one) a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status, or any other immigration status I should know about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a UK citizen, so there's no issue there.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

**Does Alex receive any benefits in his own name, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit, or is he working 24 hours or more per week?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't get any benefits in his own name, and he's not working at all - he's just at school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided, you meet all the eligibility requirements:

✓ Alex is 13 years old (under 16)
✓ You live with Alex in the UK
✓ You are responsible for him
✓ You are a UK citizen living in the UK
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
✓ Alex is not working 24+ hours per week or receiving benefits in his own name

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex. 

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. You live with Alex in the UK, which means you are responsible for him. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which is required as only one person can claim for each child. You are a UK citizen living in the UK, so you meet the residency requirements. Alex is not receiving any benefits in his own name and is not working 24 hours or more per week - he is attending school. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with their 13-year-old son Alex. They meet all the eligibility requirements: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child in the UK, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for the child, and the child is not working 24+ hours per week or receiving benefits in their own name. The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Alex is not receiving any benefits in his own name and is not working 24 hours or more per week - he is attending school. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in Manchester with their 13-year-old son Alex. They meet all the eligibility requirements: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child in the UK, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for the child, and the child is not working 24+ hours per week or receiving benefits in their own name. The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
